title: Laundry
heroAlt: Concealed laundry joinery with stacked appliances in Darlinghurst
metaTitle: Laundry Joinery Sydney | InsideOut Joinery & Renos
metaDescription: Laundry joinery across Sydney for concealed laundries, compact utility rooms, stacked appliances, stone benchtops, moisture-resistant cabinetry and better day-to-day storage.
intro: Laundry joinery works best when it is compact, durable and easy to use. Whether the laundry is a dedicated room or a hidden utility zone inside another space, the cabinetry needs to handle appliance sizing, ventilation, cleaning storage, bench space and daily wear without feeling bulky or makeshift.
included: Concealed laundries for apartments, hallways or multi-use rooms where the utility zone needs to close away neatly
Cabinetry for stacked washer and dryer layouts, side-by-side appliances and integrated tall storage
Moisture-resistant cabinetry, stone benchtops and finishes chosen for harder daily use
Overhead storage, broom cupboards, hamper storage and folding surfaces sized to the room
Laundry layouts that improve appliance access, door swings and circulation in tight footprints
Joinery that can tie into adjoining kitchens, mudrooms or bathrooms when the rooms need to feel connected

featureBlocks.title: Hidden laundry planning
featureBlocks.text: A concealed laundry only feels easy to live with when the doors open fully, the appliance clearances are right and the bench space is still useful. We plan those details from the start.

featureBlocks.title: Compact but hard-working
featureBlocks.text: Many Sydney laundries are small, so every shelf, tower and benchtop has to earn its place. The goal is not to fill the room with cupboards. It is to make the room genuinely more usable.

featureBlocks.title: Better finishes for wet areas
featureBlocks.text: Laundry cabinetry needs a tougher finish than a styling exercise. We choose materials, handles and layouts that stand up to moisture, cleaning products and daily family use.

materialsTitle: Laundry details clients often ask for
materials: Moisture-resistant cabinetry and durable internal hardware suited to wet utility spaces
Stone or stone-look benchtops where a stronger folding and utility surface is needed
Pocketing or hinged concealment details where the laundry sits inside another room
Appliance towers, tall storage and overhead cupboards to maximise limited floor space

beforeAfter.title: Before & after
beforeAfter.text: The Darlinghurst concealed laundry shows how an exposed utility nook can be wrapped in joinery so it opens properly when needed and disappears cleanly when the doors are closed.
beforeAfter.beforeAlt: Darlinghurst exposed laundry nook before concealed joinery
beforeAfter.afterAlt: Darlinghurst concealed laundry after joinery installation

faqs.question: Can you build a laundry in a very small space?
faqs.answer: Yes. Compact laundries and concealed utility zones are often where custom joinery is most valuable.

faqs.question: Can washers and dryers be stacked or hidden?
faqs.answer: Yes. We can plan stacked towers, hidden appliance cupboards and layouts that keep the room practical without looking crowded.

faqs.question: Do you include stone benchtops and splashback coordination?
faqs.answer: Yes. Where the brief calls for it, we can design the laundry joinery to suit stone benchtops and a more finished material schedule.

faqs.question: Can laundry work be part of a broader renovation?
faqs.answer: Definitely. Many clients include the laundry alongside kitchen, wardrobe or wider renovation work so the detailing stays consistent.

ctaTitle: Upgrading a laundry or hidden utility zone?
ctaText: Send through your current laundry photos or plans and we can help map out a cleaner layout, better storage and the right joinery scope.
